target.title: fix(logging): support root log file overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had a root `--log-level` override, but no matching root `--log-file` override, and the logger/status helpers did not honor `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`
- Why it matters: multi-instance or service deployments need per-process log isolation without rewriting shared config files just to move one process's file sink
- What changed: added root `--log-file <path>`, propagated it into `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE` during CLI preaction, taught logger/path helpers to honor that env override, and documented the override path
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not add log rotation, does not mutate `logging.file` in config, and does not change file-log retention or max-size behavior

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61295
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the logger already honored config-backed `logging.file`, but the root CLI startup path only promoted `--log-level`, not `--log-file`, and the shared log-path helpers had no `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE` override layer
- Missing detection / guardrail: there were no tests covering root log-file option propagation, env log-file override resolution, or daemon status reporting when the file sink was overridden out-of-band
- Contributing context (if known): several CLI/status/docs surfaces already treated log-file path as configurable, but they were only looking at config/default paths instead of the full effective override stack

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/logging/logger-env.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts`, `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: root `--log-file` should become `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`, logger resolution should prefer that env path, and daemon status should report the same effective file sink
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it validates the three boundaries that need to agree for users to trust the override: CLI parsing, runtime logger resolution, and status/reporting
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing root-option tests covered `--log-level`, but not file-path overrides
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw --log-file /path/to/openclaw.log ...` now forces the file log sink for that process.
- `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE=/path/to/openclaw.log` now overrides `logging.file`.
- Daemon status/reporting now shows the effective env-overridden log file path instead of only the config/default path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[root CLI invocation]
  -> [--log-level handled]
  -> [--log-file ignored]
  -> [logger/status fall back to config/default path]

After:
[root CLI invocation with --log-file]
  -> [preaction exports OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE]
  -> [logger + status helpers prefer env log path]
  -> [runtime and reported file sink stay aligned]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local pnpm/vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): root CLI + logger bootstrap + daemon status
- Relevant config (redacted): default config with per-process log file override via root CLI flag or `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw --log-file /tmp/custom-openclaw.log status` or export `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE=/tmp/custom-openclaw.log`.
2. Start or inspect a gateway/CLI command that writes file logs.
3. Check the resolved log file path from runtime/status surfaces.

### Expected

- The process should write logs to `/tmp/custom-openclaw.log`, and status helpers should report that same path.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no root `--log-file` path, and env-based log-file overrides were not part of the resolved logger/status path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted vitest run for `src/logging/logger-env.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/help.test.ts`, `src/infra/cli-root-options.test.ts`, `src/cli/argv.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/route-args.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/routes.test.ts`, `src/cli/run-main.test.ts`, and `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked: root option parsing with separate and `=` forms, env override precedence, Vitest silent-file fast path with explicit env file, daemon status surfacing the effective path
- What you did **not** verify: full repo-wide `pnpm check` / `pnpm test`, a live manual CLI run outside the test harness, or log rotation behavior beyond the existing code path

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: another surface could still report only config/default log paths and drift from runtime
  - Mitigation: `resolveConfiguredLogFilePath()` now includes the env override and daemon status has a dedicated regression test
- Risk: root-option parsing could regress fast-path commands that skip bootstrap work
  - Mitigation: argv/root-option/run-main route tests were extended to cover `--log-file` alongside the existing `--log-level` path

AI-assisted: Yes (Codex). Testing: targeted vitest coverage listed above. I attempted `codex review --base origin/main`, but the local Codex CLI is currently rate-limited.
